requirement: Choose one route before spending: OE/reproduction purchase or local fabrication. Do not mix OE/repro body-mount cushions with locally fabricated cushions in the same mount family.
acceptance_criteria: One signed route decision recorded before production PO; non-selected route marked do-not-order.
verification: Check body_mount_release_actions.csv and body_mount_order_release_specs.csv before placing order.
applies_to: all body-mount rubber and related stack parts
notes: Prevents duplicate purchase and mixed compression behavior.

requirement: Fabricator must manufacture only from the released revision of the spec pack: drawing/doc date, CSV part ID, and release dimensions filled from the measurement closure table.
acceptance_criteria: Quote may use photo nominal dimensions; production must use release_value_mm where the closure table marks released.
verification: Compare shop traveler/quote to rubber_recreation_measurement_closure.csv and rubber_recreation_fabrication_specs.csv.
applies_to: BM-SM; BM-LG; FS-OVAL; FS-STRIP-L; FS-STRIP-R; BM-SLV; BM-CUP
notes: Photo dimensions remain quote/prototype values until physical measurement closes.

requirement: Make first articles before full batch: one BM-SM, one BM-LG, one FS-OVAL, and one left/right strip trial if the strip route is selected.
acceptance_criteria: No full production until first articles pass dimensional check, durometer check, stack dry-fit, and vehicle/carrier fit check.
verification: Owner/mechanic inspection against closure table and dry-stack notes.
notes: First article can be made from quote dimensions but must not become full production without release signoff.

requirement: All pieces in one rubber family must come from one compound batch and one cure/cutting setup.
acceptance_criteria: Hardness spread within each family <=5 Shore A points; no visible color/texture difference across matched pieces.
verification: Durometer readings and visual inspection by part family.
applies_to: BM-SM set; BM-LG pair; FS-OVAL pair; FS-STRIP-L/R pair
notes: Keep left/right and paired stations matched.

requirement: Use new black automotive mount-grade solid rubber only: EPDM or NR/SBR for body/front-support mounts unless a measured original sample proves a different compound.
acceptance_criteria: No reclaimed tyre rubber, crumb rubber, sponge foam, mixed offcuts, used rubber, old salvage rubber, or unidentified compound.
verification: Supplier declaration plus cut-face visual inspection.
notes: These parts are not fuel-wetted seals. Fuel-wetted seals require NBR/FKM under the separate rubber ordering spec.

requirement: Hardness target is Shore A 60 +/-5 measured after cure and at least 24 hours at room temperature.
acceptance_criteria: Each checked part family must read 55-65 Shore A; preferred target 58-62 Shore A for matched pairs.
verification: ASTM D2240 Shore A durometer or equivalent calibrated Shore A gauge.
notes: Do not mix polyurethane with rubber in these mount positions.

requirement: Compound must resist permanent compression set for static body-mount service.
acceptance_criteria: Target <=25 percent; reject if >30 percent after 22 h at 70 C and 25 percent deflection, unless a better OE-equivalent compound certificate is supplied.
verification: ASTM D395 Method B test coupon from same compound batch, or supplier certificate from the compound vendor.
notes: If local fabricator cannot test this, require written compound data from the rubber stock supplier.

requirement: Compound must not be brittle or low-strength filler-heavy rubber.
acceptance_criteria: Minimum tensile strength 8 MPa and elongation at break 250 percent on batch data; higher is preferred for mount service.
verification: ASTM D412 supplier certificate or rubber stock datasheet.
notes: This screens out cheap hard filler rubber that may pass durometer but crack in service.

requirement: Compound must tolerate engine-bay and underbody heat without hardening, cracking, or becoming tacky.
acceptance_criteria: After 70 h at 70 C: no cracking or tackiness; hardness change within +/-10 Shore A points; tensile/elongation retained at least 70 percent if data is available.
verification: ASTM D573 supplier certificate or batch data; visual check after simple heated sample hold if no lab data is available.
notes: Pakistan summer heat and engine-bay soak make heat aging more important than appearance.

requirement: Compound must be exterior/weather suitable and ozone resistant.
acceptance_criteria: No visible surface cracking on stretched sample per supplier data, or written confirmation that the compound is automotive exterior/mount grade with antiozonant package.
verification: ASTM D1149 certificate where available; otherwise compound vendor written declaration.
notes: EPDM is preferred for weathering; NR/SBR must include antiozonant protection.

requirement: Steel sleeves, cup washers, shims, and any inserts must be corrosion protected after cutting/forming.
acceptance_criteria: No bare steel left on installed parts; deburred edges; zinc plate, epoxy primer, or paint fully covers cut edges.
verification: Visual inspection before assembly; check burrs by hand.
applies_to: BM-SLV; BM-CUP; BM-SHIM; FS-OVAL insert if used
notes: Protect after cutting and forming, not before.

requirement: Final dimensions must be measured after curing, trimming, drilling/punching, and 24 h relaxation.
acceptance_criteria: All inspected dimensions within the released tolerance table; rubber thickness measured with light contact pressure, not clamped hard.
verification: Caliper readings recorded on inspection sheet; ASTM D3767 used as reference where available.
notes: Rubber dimensions move after cutting and cure; inspect the finished part.

requirement: Circular cushions must have flat parallel faces and concentric bore/register geometry.
acceptance_criteria: Face parallelism <=0.5 mm across the part; bore/register concentricity <=1.0 mm; no wedge-shaped cushion.
verification: Caliper height readings at four clock positions; visual centering check and bore measurement at two axes.
notes: Wedge or off-centre cushions will force body alignment.

requirement: Rubber holes and slots must be punched, bored, or waterjet cut cleanly. Do not burn, hand-tear, or rough knife the functional holes.
acceptance_criteria: Hole/slot location within +/-0.5 mm where specified; edges clean with no radial tear starting points; hole axis square to face within practical shop capability.
verification: Pin gauge/bolt trial fit and visual inspection.
notes: Functional holes control fit. Rough holes become crack starters.

requirement: External edges must be smooth with small radius/chamfer where specified. No sharp torn edges on load-bearing rubber.
acceptance_criteria: Circular cushion outer edge radius R2-R3; relief corners R3 on FS-OVAL; no feathered flash >0.5 mm on seating faces.
verification: Visual and radius template check.
notes: Sharp internal corners and flash can tear under compression.

requirement: Rubber must be fully cured and dimensionally stable before delivery.
acceptance_criteria: No tacky surface, uncured smell, oily bleeding, voids, cracks, or trapped debris; part holds shape after 24 h at room temperature.
verification: Visual/tactile inspection and supplier cure declaration.
notes: Reject undercured pieces even if dimensions look correct.

requirement: Strip rubbers must be made from physical templates, not the photo dimensions alone.
acceptance_criteria: Card/acetate template marked left/right, vehicle orientation, hole centres from metal carrier, and final outline signed before cutting.
verification: Compare template to old strip and carrier; dry-fit on carrier before bonding or final delivery.
notes: The old strip photos are too curled/torn for final cut patterns.

requirement: If any strip rubber is bonded to a steel carrier, bond only to cleaned and prepared metal.
acceptance_criteria: Carrier blasted or sanded to clean metal, degreased, primed/bonded with rubber-to-metal adhesive, clamped flat through cure; no adhesive applied over rust, paint, oil, or loose old rubber.
verification: Shop process record, visual check before bonding, and edge lift check after cure.
applies_to: FS-STRIP-L; FS-STRIP-R; FS-OVAL insert if bonded
notes: Chemlok 205/220 or a local equivalent is acceptable if used per adhesive maker instructions.

requirement: Bonded rubber must not lift from the carrier at edges or around holes.
acceptance_criteria: No visible gaps, bubbles, loose corners, or peel at edges after hand flex; production witness coupon preferred if fabricator can make one.
verification: Visual inspection and light hand peel at non-critical edge; ASTM D429 reference if tested by lab.
notes: Do not accept cosmetic glue-over repairs.

requirement: Sleeves must be cut only after rubber/cup stack height and target compression are released.
acceptance_criteria: Length equals released free stack minus 3-4 mm target compression unless dry-fit proves a different value; ends square within 0.5 mm; ID clears M10 bolt.
verification: Dry-stack measurement and bolt pass-through test.
notes: Sleeve length controls over-crush and final tub height.

requirement: Cup/seat washers must support the rubber face and centre the stack without rocking.
acceptance_criteria: Cup OD matches cushion family, hole 11 mm for M10 clearance, dish/register depth 2-3 mm, steel 2.5-3.0 mm, no rocking on flat plate.
verification: Caliper check, flat plate check, M10 bolt trial fit.
notes: Thin flat generic washers are not substitutes for seat cups.

requirement: Dry-stack one large station, one small station, and one front-support station before final acceptance.
acceptance_criteria: Bolt passes freely; sleeve bottoms before rubber is crushed beyond target; cup seats flat; stack height matches release sheet; no side-load or rocking.
verification: Bench dry-stack with actual bolt, sleeve, cup, rubber, shim, and landing surface where possible.
notes: This is the main practical acceptance test.

requirement: Final batch acceptance requires vehicle/carrier fit, not just bench dimensions.
acceptance_criteria: Tub/front support aligns without forced bolts; left/right heights are matched; door/front clip alignment can be adjusted by shims at original interfaces only.
verification: Mechanic dry-fit notes and photos.
notes: No final torque until all stations and shims are accepted.

requirement: Fabricator must deliver a one-page inspection report with the batch.
acceptance_criteria: Report lists part ID, quantity, material batch, Shore A readings, key dimensions, visual result, date, and fabricator contact.
verification: Check report against delivered labels and parts.
notes: A handwritten sheet is acceptable if complete.

requirement: Inspect every part for visual defects and critical dimensions; record at least one full dimensional check per family plus any first article.
acceptance_criteria: No part outside released tolerance; any failed dimension requires sorting the whole family.
verification: Caliper inspection against release table.
notes: For small quantity batches, 100 percent inspection is practical.

requirement: Check hardness on representative flat area or test coupon from each family.
acceptance_criteria: At least 3 readings per family or coupon; average within 55-65 Shore A and individual readings not outside 53-67 unless surface geometry explains variation.
verification: Shore A durometer check.
notes: If the finished part is too small/curved for reliable hardness, use a same-batch flat coupon.

requirement: Package by part ID and station/side; keep old samples separate and return them.
acceptance_criteria: Each bag labeled with part ID, qty, date, rubber batch, orientation where relevant, and matching old sample/photo reference.
verification: Receiving check before leaving fabricator.
notes: Avoid mixing small/large pieces or left/right strips during transport.

requirement: Protect finished rubber from heat, oil, fuel, direct sun, and deformation before installation.
acceptance_criteria: Parts delivered clean, dry, uncompressed, and not tied with wire or sharp straps; no oily bags or solvent contact.
verification: Visual receiving check.
notes: Do not store under heavy parts in the workshop.

requirement: Reject any part with defects that would reduce strength, sealing, or seating.
acceptance_criteria: Reject cracks, cuts, voids over 1 mm at load face, exposed crumb/filler lumps, delamination, tackiness, oil bleed, embedded metal/debris, rough functional holes, mixed hardness, or wrong material.
verification: 100 percent visual inspection.
notes: The cheapest rejection point is before installation.

requirement: Fabricator may not substitute material, hardness, construction, split-stack design, sleeve length, or hole pattern without written approval.
acceptance_criteria: Any deviation must be written on the quote/invoice and approved before manufacture.
verification: Compare quote, invoice, inspection sheet, and delivered parts.
notes: This protects against local shop substitutions after verbal discussion.
